A1.title: Phase 1: A1 (Beginner)
A1.description: Goal: Survive basic daily situations. Establish pronunciation, basic nouns, articles, and regular present tense.

units.title: Alphabet & Pronunciation
units.grammar: Italian alphabet, vowel sounds, double consonants (caffè, penna), stress patterns, and common pronunciation rules (e.g. C and G before vowels).
units.vocabulary: Greetings (Ciao, Buongiorno, Arrivederci), spellings of names, nationalities, and basic numbers 1-20.

examples.italian: Come si scrive? C-A-F-F-È.
examples.english: How do you write it? C-A-F-F-E.

examples.italian: Buonasera, mi chiamo Giovanni.
examples.english: Good evening, my name is Giovanni.

units.speakingPrompt: Spell your name out loud in Italian and introduce yourself using standard greeting expressions.
units.writingPrompt: Write down a transcript of a brief conversation between two people meeting for the first time, greeting each other and spelling their names.

units.title: Articles, Gender & Number
units.grammar: Definite articles (il, lo, la, i, gli, le) and indefinite articles (un, uno, una, un'). Noun endings singular to plural (-o/-i, -a/-e, -e/-i). Noun-adjective agreement.
units.vocabulary: Common objects, colors, and basic descriptions.

examples.italian: Lo studente ha un libro rosso.
examples.english: The student has a red book.

examples.italian: Le penne nere sono sul tavolo.
examples.english: The black pens are on the table.

units.speakingPrompt: Describe three things in your room, stating their colors and whether they are singular or plural.
units.writingPrompt: Write a short list of 5 items you see in an office, using their correct definite articles (e.g., 'lo schermo, la sedia...').

units.title: Present Indicative of Essere, Avere & Regular Verbs
units.grammar: Present tense indicative of the auxiliary verbs 'essere' (to be) and 'avere' (to have) and regular verbs ending in -are, -ere, -ire (with/without -isc).
units.vocabulary: Daily activities, jobs/occupations, and numbers 20-100.

examples.italian: Loro sono ingegneri e hanno trenta anni.
examples.english: They are engineers and they are thirty years old.

examples.italian: Io parlo l'italiano e capisco bene il francese.
examples.english: I speak Italian and understand French well.

units.speakingPrompt: State your age, occupation, and talk about two things you do every day using regular verbs.
units.writingPrompt: Write a short paragraph (4 sentences) explaining your profession and what languages you study.

units.title: Adjectives, Possessives & Demonstratives
units.grammar: Adjectives agreement. Possessive adjectives (mio, tuo, suo, nostro, vostro, loro) and their articles. Demonstratives (questo, quello).
units.vocabulary: Family members, descriptions of relatives (alto, giovane, simpatico), and pets.

examples.italian: Il mio cane è molto simpatico.
examples.english: My dog is very friendly.

examples.italian: Questa è mia sorella e quello è suo figlio.
examples.english: This is my sister and that is her son.

units.speakingPrompt: Describe your family members or close friends, explaining their relations to you and their personality traits.
units.writingPrompt: Write a short paragraph introducing your family, using possessives correctly (e.g. 'mio padre', 'mia madre', 'i miei fratelli').

units.title: Prepositions, Dates & Time
units.grammar: Simple prepositions (di, a, da, in, con, su, per, tra, fra). Asking/telling dates, time ('Che ore sono?'), and days of the week.
units.vocabulary: Days of the week, months, seasons, transportation methods (in treno, in macchina), and places in town.

examples.italian: Vado a Roma in treno alle nove di mattina.
examples.english: I am going to Rome by train at nine in the morning.

examples.italian: Oggi è lunedì primo giugno.
examples.english: Today is Monday, June first.

units.speakingPrompt: Explain your weekly schedule: what days you study, what time you wake up, and how you travel to work/school.
units.writingPrompt: Draft an email invitation to a friend for dinner. State the date, time, and location using prepositions.

units.title: Basic Writing & Everyday Situations
units.grammar: Consolidation of A1 grammar. Simple sentence construction, negative sentences (non + verb).
units.vocabulary: Food, shopping expressions, buying groceries, ordering in a cafe.

examples.italian: Vorrei un cornetto e un cappuccino, per favore.
examples.english: I would like a croissant and a cappuccino, please.

examples.italian: Quanto costa questo chilo di mele?
examples.english: How much does this kilo of apples cost?

units.speakingPrompt: Roleplay ordering breakfast at an Italian bar. Ask for pastries, coffee, and request the bill.
units.writingPrompt: Write a message describing your typical day from breakfast to evening routine (50-80 words).

A2.title: Phase 2: A2 (Elementary)
A2.description: Goal: Handle everyday conversations. Expand vocabulary, talk about past experiences, use reflexives and direct/indirect pronouns.

units.title: The Past Tense (Passato Prossimo)
units.grammar: Passato Prossimo conjugation. Choosing 'essere' vs. 'avere' auxiliaries. Past participle agreement rules with 'essere' (e.g., 'siamo andati/e').
units.vocabulary: Travel terms, hotel bookings, past time expressions (ieri, fa, scorso).

examples.italian: L'anno scorso siamo andati in vacanza in Italia.
examples.english: Last year we went on vacation to Italy.

examples.italian: Ho visto un bel film al cinema sabato scorso.
examples.english: I saw a nice movie at the cinema last Saturday.

units.speakingPrompt: Tell a story about what you did on your last vacation. Where did you stay and what did you eat?
units.writingPrompt: Write a postcard to a friend detailing your arrival in an Italian city and what monuments you visited yesterday.

units.title: Reflexive Verbs & Routines
units.grammar: Conjugating reflexive verbs (svegliarsi, vestirsi, divertirsi) in present and past tenses. Position of reflexive pronouns.
units.vocabulary: Daily personal care, clothing, body parts, and feelings.

examples.italian: Mi sveglio alle sette e mi vesto subito.
examples.english: I wake up at seven and get dressed immediately.

examples.italian: Ieri mi sono annoiato molto alla riunione.
examples.english: Yesterday I was very bored at the meeting.

units.speakingPrompt: Explain your morning and evening routines in detail, mentioning at least four reflexive verbs.
units.writingPrompt: Describe a day in the life of a friend or family member, focusing on their morning preparation and habits.

units.title: Direct & Indirect Object Pronouns
units.grammar: Direct object pronouns (mi, ti, lo, la, ci, vi, li, le) and indirect object pronouns (mi, ti, gli, le, ci, vi, loro). Pronoun placement with verbs.
units.vocabulary: Dining at restaurants, shopping for clothes, and interactions.

examples.italian: La pizza? L'ho mangiata ieri.
examples.english: The pizza? I ate it yesterday.

examples.italian: Le ho telefonato ieri per invitarla.
examples.english: I called her yesterday to invite her.

units.speakingPrompt: Describe what you would buy for a friend's birthday and explain why you chose it, using pronouns.
units.writingPrompt: Write a dialogue between a shop assistant and a client trying on and buying clothes.

units.title: Modal Verbs & Expressing Needs
units.grammar: Modal verbs: potere (can), volere (want), dovere (must), and sapere (know how to). Using modals in past tenses.
units.vocabulary: Health, expressing symptoms, pharmacy visits, and emergency expressions.

examples.italian: Oggi non posso uscire perché devo studiare.
examples.english: Today I cannot go out because I must study.

examples.italian: Non ho potuto finire il lavoro ieri sera.
examples.english: I was not able to finish the work last night.

units.speakingPrompt: Explain a situation where you had to cancel plans due to illness or responsibilities, using modal verbs.
units.writingPrompt: Write a doctor's note or an email explaining why you cannot attend work or school tomorrow.

units.title: Comparatives & Superlatives
units.grammar: Comparatives of majority, minority, and equality (più/meno... di/che, come, quanto). Absolute and relative superlatives. Irregular forms (migliore, ottimo).
units.vocabulary: Geography, cities, describing places, weather, and climate.

examples.italian: Milano è più fredda di Palermo ma meno piovosa di Londra.
examples.english: Milan is colder than Palermo but less rainy than London.

examples.italian: Questo ristorante prepara la pasta migliore della città.
examples.english: This restaurant makes the best pasta in town.

units.speakingPrompt: Compare the weather and lifestyle of your hometown with an Italian city of your choice.
units.writingPrompt: Write a comparative review of two hotels or services you used during a trip.

units.title: Intro to Imperfect & Future Tenses
units.grammar: Introduction to conjugations of Imperfetto (past descriptions) and Futuro Semplice (future projections).
units.vocabulary: Hobbies, sports, describing weather, and simple predictions.

examples.italian: Da giovane leggevo molti libri di avventura.
examples.english: When I was young, I read many adventure books.

examples.italian: Domani farà bel tempo in tutta Italia.
examples.english: Tomorrow the weather will be nice across Italy.

units.speakingPrompt: Contrast what you used to do in your free time in the past with what you plan to do next weekend.
units.writingPrompt: Write a paragraph describing a childhood memory, setting the scene with imperfect verbs.

B1.title: Phase 3: B1 Foundation
B1.description: Goal: Function independently. Master past narration, relative pronouns, conditionals, particles Ci/Ne, and professional terminology.

units.title: Imperfetto & Past Contrasts
units.grammar: Mastery of Imperfetto. Choosing between Passato Prossimo (completed actions) and Imperfetto (background states, habits, weather) in complex narrations.
units.vocabulary: School and university life, subjects, childhood memories.

examples.italian: Mentre dormivo, il telefono ha squillato all'improvviso.
examples.english: While I was sleeping, the phone suddenly rang.

examples.italian: Quando andavo a scuola, studiavo sempre il pomeriggio.
examples.english: When I went to school, I always studied in the afternoon.

units.speakingPrompt: Tell a story about an unexpected event that happened to you at school or university.
units.writingPrompt: Write a narrative essay (120-150 words) about a memorable day in your childhood using both past tenses.

units.title: Future Tense Mastery
units.grammar: Conjugating and using Futuro Semplice and Futuro Anteriore (future perfect). Expressing hypothetical future scenarios and assumptions.
units.vocabulary: Jobs, recruitment, working environments, and professional career goals.

examples.italian: Appena avrò completato il progetto, farò una presentazione.
examples.english: As soon as I have completed the project, I will make a presentation.

examples.italian: Forse avranno dimenticato l'appuntamento.
examples.english: Perhaps they forgot the appointment (future of probability).

units.speakingPrompt: Describe where you will work, live, and how you will use Italian five years from now.
units.writingPrompt: Write an application summary outlining your future qualifications and career development plans.

units.title: Combined Pronouns
units.grammar: Forming combined pronouns (me lo, te la, glielo, ce li, ve le, etc.). Position with respect to regular verbs and past participles.
units.vocabulary: Office communications, managing assignments, banking, and financial services.

examples.italian: Hai mandato i documenti al direttore? Sì, glieli ho spediti ieri.
examples.english: Did you send the documents to the director? Yes, I sent them to him yesterday.

examples.italian: Se hai bisogno del libro, te lo porto stasera.
examples.english: If you need the book, I'll bring it to you tonight.

units.speakingPrompt: Explain how you managed a task or processed documentation at work using combined pronouns.
units.writingPrompt: Write a dialogue between a bank employee and a customer discussing loan documents using double pronouns.

units.title: Relative Pronouns
units.grammar: Subject and object relative pronouns: 'che', 'cui' (preceded by prepositions), 'chi' (he who/whoever), and 'quello che/ciò che'.
units.vocabulary: Housing search, renting apartments, and administrative processes.

examples.italian: La casa in cui abito è molto luminosa.
examples.english: The house in which I live is very bright.

examples.italian: Ciò che mi preoccupa di più è il contratto di affitto.
examples.english: What worries me the most is the rental contract.

units.speakingPrompt: Describe your ideal apartment, specifying details using relative pronouns.
units.writingPrompt: Draft an email to a real estate agent asking for information on a property, describing the features you require.

units.title: The Present Conditional Mood
units.grammar: Conjugation of Condizionale Presente. Expressing polite requests, advice, desires, and hypothetical situations.
units.vocabulary: Healthcare, booking medical appointments, talking to a doctor, and descriptions of symptoms.

examples.italian: Mi piacerebbe parlare con il dottore, se possibile.
examples.english: I would like to speak with the doctor, if possible.

examples.italian: Dovresti riposare di più ed evitare sforzi.
examples.english: You should rest more and avoid exertion.

units.speakingPrompt: Roleplay calling a clinic. Describe symptoms politely and request a convenient appointment.
units.writingPrompt: Write an advice column response suggesting lifestyle changes for a reader dealing with stress.

units.title: Particles Ci & Ne
units.grammar: Locative 'ci' and partitive/specifying 'ne'. Position with simple and compound tenses. Idiomatic uses (e.g. farcela, andarsene).
units.vocabulary: Local administration, residency applications, public services, and permits.

examples.italian: Sei andato in questura? Sì, ci vado adesso.
examples.english: Did you go to the police headquarters? Yes, I am going there now.

examples.italian: Quanti moduli ti servono? Ne servono tre.
examples.english: How many forms do you need? I need three of them.

units.speakingPrompt: Explain the process of registering at a city hall, specifying quantities and locations using ci and ne.
units.writingPrompt: Write a note to a colleague explaining how to submit expense receipts, using 'ci' and 'ne' correctly.

B1_plus.title: Phase 4: B1+ Advanced Foundation
B1_plus.description: Goal: Speak comfortably about complex topics. Master the present/past subjunctive, passive constructions, reported speech, and gerunds.

units.title: Present Subjunctive (Congiuntivo Presente)
units.grammar: Forming Congiuntivo Presente. Identifying triggers: verbs of opinion (pensare, credere), emotion (sperare, temere), and doubt.
units.vocabulary: Social changes, civil rights, and community development.

examples.italian: Spero che il governo prenda una decisione corretta.
examples.english: I hope the government makes a correct decision.

examples.italian: Credo che l'italiano sia una lingua melodica.
examples.english: I believe that Italian is a melodic language.

units.speakingPrompt: Express your opinions on how social media is changing relationships, using 'Credo che...', 'Penso che...'.
units.writingPrompt: Write an opinion paragraph (100-120 words) on the importance of local community involvement.

units.title: Past Subjunctive (Congiuntivo Passato)
units.grammar: Forming Congiuntivo Passato. Subjunctive tense agreement rules in sentences referring to past events.
units.vocabulary: Political updates, local elections, and government policies.

examples.italian: Temo che abbiano preso la decisione sbagliata.
examples.english: I fear that they made the wrong decision.

examples.italian: È strano che non sia ancora arrivato nessuno.
examples.english: It is strange that nobody has arrived yet.

units.speakingPrompt: Express your thoughts on a recent local election or news item. Did the public make the right choice?
units.writingPrompt: Write a short report summarizing a past debate and sharing your evaluation of the arguments.

units.title: Reported Speech (Discorso Indiretto)
units.grammar: Tense changes when converting direct speech to reported speech. Transitions from present/past to future-in-the-past.
units.vocabulary: Media, news reports, journalism, and quoting sources.

examples.italian: Ha detto che sarebbe venuto il giorno dopo.
examples.english: He said that he would come the next day.

examples.italian: Il giornalista ha annunciato che l'accordo era firmato.
examples.english: The journalist announced that the agreement was signed.

units.speakingPrompt: Report what an Italian news reporter or politician stated in a recent interview.
units.writingPrompt: Write a news summary reporting on statements made by local community leaders.

units.title: The Passive Voice
units.grammar: Forming passive sentences using 'essere' and 'venire'. Expressing necessity using 'andare' + past participle (e.g., 'va fatto').
units.vocabulary: Environmental policies, ecology, and climate change debates.

examples.italian: Questo problema va risolto al più presto.
examples.english: This problem must be solved as soon as possible.

examples.italian: Molte foreste vengono distrutte ogni anno.
examples.english: Many forests are destroyed every year.

units.speakingPrompt: Present a solution to a local pollution issue using passive constructions to emphasize actions.
units.writingPrompt: Write an article describing the actions taken by local organizations to reduce energy consumption.

units.title: The Gerund (Gerundio)
units.grammar: Present and past gerunds. Expressing implicit clauses of cause, time, condition, or concession (e.g. 'pur sapendo').
units.vocabulary: Economic terms, inflation, market trends, and finance.

examples.italian: Pur avendo studiato, non ricordavo le risposte.
examples.english: Despite having studied, I didn't remember the answers.

examples.italian: Lavorando costantemente, otterrai grandi risultati.
examples.english: By working constantly, you will achieve great results.

units.speakingPrompt: Explain how you overcame a major difficulty by utilizing gerund clauses.
units.writingPrompt: Write a column assessing the state of the local economy, utilizing gerunds to link causes and results.

units.title: Current Events Debate
units.grammar: Synthesizing B1+ grammar rules. Structuring comparisons, pros and cons, and logical progressions.
units.vocabulary: Immigration, cultural diversity, and social integration.

examples.italian: Da un lato penso che sia necessario, dall'altro temo le conseguenze.
examples.english: On one hand I think it is necessary, on the other hand I fear the consequences.

examples.italian: Si dice che l'integrazione culturale arricchisca la società.
examples.english: It is said that cultural integration enriches society.

units.speakingPrompt: Debate the pros and cons of studying or working abroad vs. staying in one's home country.
units.writingPrompt: Write a short essay (150-200 words) discussing how cities can facilitate integration for new residents.

B2.title: Phase 5: B2 Core
B2.description: Goal: University-ready Italian. Master advanced subjunctive, past conditional, hypothetical periods, and academic connectors.

units.title: Advanced Subjunctive (Imperfetto & Trapassato)
units.grammar: Conjugating and using Congiuntivo Imperfetto and Trapassato. Correct sequence of tenses (consecutio temporum) with verbs of emotion/opinion in the past.
units.vocabulary: University administration, research vocabulary, lecture descriptions.

examples.italian: Pensavo che tu fossi già iscritto all'università.
examples.english: I thought that you had already enrolled in university.

examples.italian: Sarebbe stato fantastico se avessimo superato quell'esame.
examples.english: It would have been fantastic if we had passed that exam.

units.speakingPrompt: Talk about a university course or research topic you would have liked to study in the past.
units.writingPrompt: Write an email to a university registrar explaining a past administrative problem, using correct past subjunctive forms.

units.title: The Past Conditional (Condizionale Passato)
units.grammar: Conjugating and using Condizionale Passato. Expressing actions that could have occurred in the past but didn't, or future actions relative to past moments.
units.vocabulary: Business structures, project management, and reporting errors.

examples.italian: Il direttore avrebbe voluto organizzare una conferenza stampa.
examples.english: The director would have wanted to organize a press conference.

examples.italian: Ci hai assicurato che avresti spedito i contratti.
examples.english: You assured us that you would send the contracts.

units.speakingPrompt: Explain a professional project that failed or was delayed, describing what *should* have happened.
units.writingPrompt: Write a memo analyzing a project delay, outlining what actions would have improved the outcome.

units.title: Hypothetical Periods (Periodo Ipotetico)
units.grammar: Mastering the three types of hypothetical sentences: reality (indicative + indicative), possibility (subjunctive imperfetto + conditional present), and unreality (subjunctive trapassato + conditional passato). Mixed conditionals.
units.vocabulary: Speculation, environmental sustainability, climate solutions.

examples.italian: Se avessimo ridotto le emissioni prima, oggi la situazione sarebbe migliore.
examples.english: If we had reduced emissions earlier, today the situation would be better.

examples.italian: Se potessi viaggiare indietro nel tempo, studierei a Bologna.
examples.english: If I could travel back in time, I would study in Bologna.

units.speakingPrompt: What would you do to tackle climate change if you were a government leader? Use hypothetical sentences.
units.writingPrompt: Write a speculative essay (200 words) on how city planning would change if private cars were banned.

units.title: Logical Connectors & Argumentation
units.grammar: Using advanced logical connectors: tuttavia (however), sebbene/nonostante (although), inoltre (furthermore), dunque/pertanto (therefore), di conseguenza (consequently), malgrado (despite), affinché (so that), qualora (in case).
units.vocabulary: Marketing, data analysis, technology adoption.

examples.italian: Sebbene l'azienda abbia registrato perdite, gli investimenti continueranno.
examples.english: Although the company registered losses, investments will continue.

examples.italian: Qualora si presentassero anomalie, il sistema si bloccherà.
examples.english: Should anomalies present themselves, the system will lock.

units.speakingPrompt: Present arguments supporting or opposing data collection by technology firms, using at least 5 different connectors.
units.writingPrompt: Write an analysis of a market trend, detailing actions and outcomes using connectors of concession and consequence.

units.title: Complex Relative Clauses & Academic Speech
units.grammar: Structuring complex relative clauses (e.g. 'il cui scopo', 'dopo il quale'). Nominalization (replacing verbs with noun phrases to sound academic).
units.vocabulary: Lectures, academic examinations, researching methodologies.

examples.italian: Il progetto, il cui scopo è promuovere l'ecologia, inizierà a maggio.
examples.english: The project, whose purpose is to promote ecology, will start in May.

examples.italian: La rimozione delle barriere architettoniche favorisce l'inclusione.
examples.english: The removal of architectural barriers promotes inclusion.

units.speakingPrompt: Explain a scientific or sociological theory you are familiar with, using formal nominalizations.
units.writingPrompt: Write an abstract for a hypothetical research paper, summarizing its thesis and methodologies (150 words).

units.title: Professional Communications
units.grammar: Formatting letters and proposals. Using the polite third-person plural and singular structures ('Lei', 'Loro') in business context.
units.vocabulary: Business negotiations, job interview techniques, contract agreements.

examples.italian: La ringraziamo per aver accettato il nostro invito.
examples.english: We thank you (polite singular) for accepting our invitation.

examples.italian: Qualora Lei fosse interessato, restiamo a disposizione.
examples.english: Should you (polite singular) be interested, we remain at your disposal.

units.speakingPrompt: Participate in a simulated job interview in Italian. Answer questions about your strengths and goals.
units.writingPrompt: Write a formal letter applying for an internship or job opening at an Italian institution.

B2_mastery.title: Phase 6: B2 Mastery
B2_mastery.description: Goal: Sound natural, academic, and educated. Read Italian newspapers, draft academic essays, participate in debates, and handle interviews.

units.title: Reading the Italian Press
units.grammar: Syntactical analysis of journalistic prose. Understanding double negatives, passive voice, and sub-clauses in news articles.
units.vocabulary: Political jargon, economic indicators, terms from papers like Corriere della Sera, La Repubblica, Il Sole 24 Ore.

examples.italian: L'articolo solleva dubbi sulla sostenibilità delle misure fiscali.
examples.english: The article raises doubts about the sustainability of the fiscal measures.

examples.italian: Ciononostante, il consiglio dei ministri ha approvato il decreto.
examples.english: Nonetheless, the council of ministers approved the decree.

units.speakingPrompt: Summarize a recent news event reported in an Italian newspaper and share your evaluation.
units.writingPrompt: Write a summary (150 words) of an editorial piece discussing immigration policies in Europe.

units.title: Listening to Academic and Public Discussions
units.grammar: Recognizing spoken connectors, colloquial particles, speed variations, regional accents, and rhetorical structures in public lectures.
units.vocabulary: Podcasts, interviews, public panel terms, and research vocabulary.

examples.italian: A dire il vero, la questione è molto complessa.
examples.english: Truth be told, the issue is very complex.

examples.italian: Vorrei riallacciarmi a quanto detto dal relatore.
examples.english: I would like to refer back to what the speaker said.

units.speakingPrompt: Refute or support an argument made during a panel discussion on renewable energy.
units.writingPrompt: Write an opinion essay responding to a podcast discussion about the impact of remote working.

units.title: Formal Essay Writing (250-500 words)
units.grammar: Structuring formal introductions, body paragraphs with counter-arguments, and strong conclusions. Syntactical cohesion.
units.vocabulary: Transition words (in primo luogo, a tal proposito, in conclusione), academic descriptors.

examples.italian: In conclusione, emerge chiaramente la necessità di riforme.
examples.english: In conclusion, the necessity of reforms clearly emerges.

examples.italian: Si ritiene comunemente che gli investimenti siano insufficienti.
examples.english: It is commonly believed that investments are insufficient.

units.speakingPrompt: Present a 3-minute oral defense of a thesis regarding the conservation of historic monuments.
units.writingPrompt: Compose a formal essay (300 words) arguing for or against the integration of AI tools in university examinations.

units.title: Debating & Presenting Opinions
units.grammar: Phrasing agreements, disagreements, interruptions, and rebuttals politely and effectively. Using idioms to color arguments.
units.vocabulary: Idiomatic expressions (arrampicarsi sugli specchi, gettare la spugna, tagliare la testa al toro).

examples.italian: Mi dispiace interrompere, ma questo argomento non regge.
examples.english: I am sorry to interrupt, but this argument does not hold up.

examples.italian: Non arrampichiamoci sugli specchi: i dati parlano chiaro.
examples.english: Let's not make desperate excuses: the data is clear.

units.speakingPrompt: Engage in an Italian debate about the impact of tourism on historic Italian cities like Venice and Florence.
units.writingPrompt: Write an opinion piece discussing how mass tourism compromises local culture, using at least three idioms.

units.title: Professional Presentations
units.grammar: Formatting oral presentations, utilizing slides, and managing audience questions. Handling advanced polite conditionals.
units.vocabulary: Business presentations, slides description terms, handling questions.

examples.italian: Come potete vedere da questo grafico, le vendite sono stabili.
examples.english: As you can see from this graph, sales are stable.

examples.italian: Se ci sono domande, sarò felice di rispondere.
examples.english: If there are questions, I will be happy to answer.

units.speakingPrompt: Deliver a 5-minute presentation in Italian about a product or project you developed recently.
units.writingPrompt: Prepare a presentation slide script outlining the milestones of a company sustainability initiative.

units.title: Literary & Intellectual Discourse
units.grammar: Analyzing excerpts of Italian literature (e.g. Calvino, Eco). Understanding complex prose, metaphors, and narrative tenses.
units.vocabulary: Literary devices, metaphors, expressions of stylistic nuances.

examples.italian: L'opera mette in evidenza la solitudine dell'uomo moderno.
examples.english: The work highlights the solitude of modern man.

examples.italian: Questa metafora esprime la dualità dell'anima.
examples.english: This metaphor expresses the duality of the soul.

units.speakingPrompt: Discuss your interpretation of a famous Italian quote or short story with your tutor.
units.writingPrompt: Write a critical analysis (200 words) of a short excerpt of modern Italian literature.
